data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise Ahead of Fed Minutes, NVDA and Tech Stocks Gain
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended higher on Wednesday morning ahead of the minutes from the Federal Reserve's January meeting. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.49%, 0.39%, and 0.26%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EST on February 18.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed higher.  The S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq Composite all edged up roughly 0.1% on the day. The software sector, battered by AI disruption fears, dropped further yesterday. CrowdStrike (CRWD) tumbled 3.6%, while ServiceNow (NOW) shed 1.1%.

Turning to economic data, traders are focused on the FOMC minutes due later today. Also, data on housing starts, building permits, and durable goods orders will be published today. Of more importance is Friday's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index for December, the Fed's favorite gauge, which will reveal more about the economy's health.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) were up 2% in pre-market trading, driven by its expanded partnership with Meta Platforms (META). Meanwhile, Palo Alto Networks (PANW) stock fell 6% after issuing weak earnings guidance for the current quarter.

Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stock was up 3% in pre-market trading after Mizuho upgraded the AI-powered data analytics company to Buy from Hold, citing an improved risk-reward set-up. In contrast, Analog Devices (ADI) stock was up about 6% after reporting better-than-expected revenue and earnings for Q1 FY26.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Meta Stock Wobbles as Mark Zuckerberg Testifies in Social Media Addiction Trial
data.description:
Tech giant Meta's (META) shares were flat in Wednesday's pre-market trading as CEO Mark Zuckerberg prepared to be questioned in a landmark social media addiction trial that began earlier this month.

The case is the first such lawsuit against a social media company to push through to the trial stage. The result of the trial could set a precedent for how platforms are held accountable for the impact of posted content, especially on children and teenagers.

Zuckerberg is expected to point to actions Meta has taken to make its platforms, including Instagram, safe for young people.

data.title: Uber Stock Ticks Up as Company Pledges $100 Million for Robotaxi Charging Hubs
data.description:
Uber Technologies (UBER) shares rose 0.68% to $71.01 in premarket trading on Wednesday. This small jump follows news that the company will spend $100 million on a new network of charging stations for robotaxis. Uber plans to build these high-power hubs in the San Francisco Bay Area, Los Angeles, and Dallas. The goal is to make autonomous fleets more efficient by keeping cars on the road longer. Owning the chargers helps Uber lower costs and ensures that self-driving cars spend less time waiting for a battery boost and more time picking up riders.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Uber Tackles the Robotaxi Skeptics

This big investment is an attempt to prove that Uber has a bright future even as robots replace human drivers. Some investors worry that companies like Waymo will push Uber aside, but Uber is fighting back by becoming an essential partner. Instead of making its own cars, Uber is teaming up with brands like Lucid (LCID), Nuro, and Volkswagen (VWAGY). These partners build the tech, while Uber handles the hard work of running the fleet. The company wants to be the "manager" that keeps everything running smoothly, which is a role that's hard for others to copy.

Uber Aims for a Massive Expansion into 10 Cities by Year-End

Uber is not stopping with just chargers and has a goal to offer driverless rides in at least 10 cities by the end of 2026. This year, the company will launch Nuro and Lucid robotaxis in the Bay Area and Volkswagen self-driving vans in Los Angeles. Uber has already shown this works in Austin and Atlanta, where it helps manage Waymo vehicles on the ground. The company is working on managing the messy parts of owning a fleet by handling cleaning, inspections, and power grid hookups to ensure it stays the top platform for the whole robotaxi world.

Is Uber a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

On Wall Street, UBER stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 28 Buy, three Hold, and one Sell rating. In addition, UBER's average stock price target of $106.76 implies ~51% upside potential over the next 12 months, suggesting analysts see Uber as notably undervalued following its recent share price lag.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) CEO Says 'AGI Could Arrive in 5 to 8 Years' as AI Cyber Risks Grow
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) is once again putting the long-term AI story front and center. This week, Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis said at the India AI Impact Summit 2026 that artificial general intelligence, or AGI, could arrive within the next 5 to 8 years. Hassabis explained that today's AI tools are still uneven. He said they show what he called "jagged intelligence," meaning they can shine in one area but fail in another. For example, he noted that systems can win top math awards, yet still "make mistakes in elementary math if a question is posed in a certain way."

data.title: Investors Sue Ardent Health Over Accounts Receivable Claims
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against Ardent Health (ARDT) on January 7, 2026.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Plaintiffs – shareholders – in the federal securities action allege that they acquired Ardent Health stock at artificially inflated prices between July 18, 2024 and November 12, 2025, known as the "Class Period." They are now seeking compensation for financial losses that occurred upon public revelation of the company's alleged misconduct during that time.

Investors who bought Ardent stock during that period can click here to learn about joining the lawsuit.

Company Background

Ardent Health is a self-described leading provider of healthcare in growing mid-sized urban markets. It makes care available to patients through its  "comprehensive network of hospitals, ambulatory sites of care and providers." This network spans six states and purportedly includes not only primary care and specialty care clinics, but also ambulatory surgery centers, urgent care centers, and more. 

Founded in 2001, the company used to be known as Ardent Health Partners, Inc. It began trading publicly in 2024 and adopted its current moniker last year. In all, the Brentwood, Tennessee-based company says it now has more than 24,000 employees. 

Why Shareholders are Angry

The company and two of its senior officers (the "Individual Defendants") are accused of deceiving investors by lying and withholding important information about Ardent Health's business and prospects during the Class Period. 

In particular, they are accused of omitting truthful information about the company's accounts receivable, and other issues, from SEC filings and related material. By knowingly or recklessly doing so, they allegedly caused Ardent Health stock to trade at artificially inflated prices during the time in question. 

The truth, say the shareholders, came out after the market closed on November 12, 2025. That's when the company revealed a $43 million decrease in third quarter 2025 revenue. According to the complaint, the company blamed the decrease on revised determinations of accounts receivable collectability after its transition to a new revenue accounting system. The company also blamed the decrease on  purported "recently completed hindsight evaluations of historical collection trends." 

In this context, Ardent Health announced more bad news – specifically a cut to 2025 EBITDA guidance of $57.5 million at the midpoint, or about 9.6%, from $575 million – $625 million to $530 million – $555 million, because of "persistent industry-wide cost pressures," including "payer denials."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Booking (BKNG) Earnings Today – Which KPI Should You Watch?
data.description:
American travel and reservation company Booking Holdings (BKNG) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results after the market closes today, February 18. Investors should closely monitor trends in Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), as they highlight past performance and set expectations for the future. For Booking.com, Gross Travel Bookings stands out as the vital KPI, directly fueling revenue from customer bookings.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Is a KPI?

A KPI is a measurable metric, like revenue growth, margins, or daily active users (DAUs), that offers insights into trends, efficiency, and potential challenges such as market shifts or resource inefficiencies.

Why Gross Bookings Matter for BKNG

Gross Bookings tracks the total value of all travel services booked by customers (net of cancellations), signaling consumer demand and market share. The company divides its Gross Bookings into two segments:

  • Merchant Bookings: BKNG collects full payment upfront and pays suppliers (net). This model offers higher risk, more control, and better margins.
  • Agency Bookings: BKNG earns commissions only, as suppliers handle payments. This is lower risk, less control, and easier to scale.

In Q3FY25, the company's Gross Bookings increased 14% year-over-year to $49.7 billion. The company had guided for 11%–13% growth in Q4.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
